name: IFRS18-ORAS provisional codebook
methodology_note: Validation-calibrated codebook for observable documentary alignment screening only. It is not an IFRS 18 compliance opinion, audit conclusion, organisational readiness measure, proof of full IFRS 18 implementation, or proof that an automatically detected alternative performance measure is legally an IFRS 18 management-defined performance measure. External accounting review remains required before empirical deployment.

items.label: Operating profit or loss subtotal is observable
items.explanatory_note: EBIT alone is weak because composition is not automatically equivalent to IFRS 18 subtotals.

items.label: Profit or loss before financing and income taxes subtotal is observable
items.explanatory_note: EBIT alone is weak because composition is not automatically equivalent to IFRS 18 subtotals.

items.label: Operating, investing and financing categories are traceable
Income-tax expense or income is separately observable
Discontinued operations are separately observable when relevant
Equity-method associates and joint ventures result is separately observable when relevant
Finance income or costs are separately observable
Potential MPM/APM measure has a clear label
items.explanatory_note: The software detects only potential MPM candidates, not legal IFRS 18 MPM status.

items.label: Usefulness or management-view explanation is observable
Calculation method is observable
Reconciliation to an IFRS subtotal is observable
Reconciling items are disaggregated
Tax effect of reconciling items is observable
Non-controlling-interest effect is observable
Changes, additions or cessations of measures and comparative information are observable
Cross-references between line items and notes are observable
Specific explanations of material-looking other items are observable
items.explanatory_note: The software does not infer IFRS materiality; this is only a research screening signal.

items.label: Expense presentation basis is identifiable
Cost of sales is separately observable when function presentation is detected
Qualitative description of the nature of expenses included in function line items is observable
Depreciation total is observable
Amortisation total is observable
Employee-benefits total is observable
Impairment losses and reversals total is observable
Inventory write-downs and reversals total is observable
Material-disaggregation language is observable
items.explanatory_note: No IFRS materiality conclusion is inferred.

items.label: IFRS 18 is mentioned
Effective date or planned adoption timing is observable
Impact assessment or implementation project is observable
Affected reporting areas are qualitatively described
Expected qualitative or quantitative effects are disclosed
Indirect cash-flow reconciliation starts from operating profit
Interest received classified as investing
Interest paid classified as financing
Dividends received classified as investing
Dividends paid classified as financing
